Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yagawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yagawa Shrine''' (やがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan-chō Morijiri|Q86735490|WD=Q86735490|1=Kōnan-chō Morijiri}}, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|title=Yagawa Shrine|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=613|website=|accessdate=2019-10-24|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto is revered as a deity of industry and long healthy life; Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto as a deity for mastery of arts and fulfillment of wishes. Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto is also written as Yagawae-hime / Yagawae-hime. In the genealogy of Ōkuninushi in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}, there is a passage: "{{ill|lt=Kuninintomi-no-Kami|Q48759713|WD=Q48759713|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami}} married {{ill|lt=Ashinadaka-no-Kami|Q55533749|WD=Q55533749|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami}} (also called Yagawae-hime), and their child was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamika-no-Takesawaya-jinumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}." Also, in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} section of the Kojiki, Miyanushi Yagawae-hime appears as one of the emperor's consorts. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era, during construction of {{ill|lt=Shigaraki-no-Miya|Q262438|WD=Q262438|1=Shigaraki Palace}} by {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears first among eight shrines of Kōka District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, and is listed as a small shrine. A 1337 source from the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}} records it as "Soma-no-Kawa-no-Miya," and in the medieval period it gathered support from local warrior-landholders as the sōsha of Ikebara Soma-shō. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}, it was used as a meeting place of the {{ill|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō|Q85882491|WD=Q85882491|1=Kōka ikki}}, functioning as a site strengthening regional cohesion. After the decline of Kōka groups in the {{ill|lt=Kōka-yure|Q6455112|WD=Q6455112|1=Kōga-ryū}} of {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}, ritual structure changed. In {{ill|lt=1652|Q6962|WD=Q6962|1=1652}}, among ujiko villages, 9 villages became ujiko of Shingū Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōnan-chō Shinjī|1=UNKNOWN}}), 6 villages became ujiko of Hiyoshi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Minakuchi-chō Sandaiji|Q86735520|WD=Q86735520|1=Minakuchi-chō Sandaiji}}), and 7 villages (Morijiri, Fukagawa, Hietani, Kuzuraki, Terashō, Sugitani, Shiono) remained ujiko (Yagawa Hongō / Yagawa 7 villages), while another 15 villages were called external ujiko. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was a revered shrine of {{ill|lt=Minakuchi Domain|Q10863504|WD=Q10863504|1=Minakuchi Domain}}. In October {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1842|Q7625|WD=Q7625|1=1842}}), the "Kōka Disturbance" ({{ill|lt=Ōmi Tenpō Uprising|Q11638484|WD=Q11638484|lt=Tenpō uprising|1=Tenpō uprising}}) occurred at this shrine precinct, where tens of thousands of farmers gathered and rose against shogunate land survey policy. Before Meiji it was called Yagawa Daimyōjin and was a broad-area village guardian shrine with a wide ujiko sphere{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016年12月12日|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|pages=171-172}}. Rituals were carried out under {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} by Yagawadera Seijō-in, the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (now extinct). === National historic site === Precincts - designated as a national historic site as one of the {{ill|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō|Q85882491|WD=Q85882491|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō site group|1=Kōka ikki}}{{Cite journal|author=Kōka City|year=2009|title=Understanding the real figure of Kōka warriors who raced through the Sengoku period|url=http://www.city.koka.lg.jp/secure/6711/2009_0601_p02_03.pdf|journal=Kōhō Aikōka|volume=June 1 issue|page=}}. === Cultural properties === === Prefecture-designated cultural properties === * Rōmon gate - said to have been donated in thanks for rainmaking fulfilled for 50 villages in {{ill|lt=Yamabe District|Q1130739|WD=Q1130739|1=Yamabe District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (northern {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}). Originally two-storied, but said to have lost the upper story to weather. Built in {{ill|lt=1482|Q6633|WD=Q6633|1=1482}}. * Paper, light-color paintings "Immortals with game board books" and "Landscape" - by {{ill|lt=Yokoi Kinkoku|Q3122882|WD=Q3122882|1=Yokoi Kinkoku}}. === City-designated cultural properties === * Honden - sangen-sha nagare-zukuri with steel-sheet roof. Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1755|Q7627|WD=Q7627|1=1755}} by Takagi Tajima, a carpenter from Hachiman. * Stone arched bridge (taiko-bashi) - built in {{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}. Oldest stone arched bridge in Kōka{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016年12月12日|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=389}} and largest. * Wooden male deity statue - Heian-period work. === Other === * Wooden female deity statue - Heian-period work. === Gallery === ファイル:Sandō to Yagawa jinja, Koka 04.jpg|Approach ファイル:Yagawa-Shrine.jpg|Torii ファイル:Stone bridge at Yagawa jinja, Koka 05.jpg|Taiko bridge ファイル:Rōmon at Yagawa jinja, Koka 02.jpg|Rōmon ファイル:Yagawa jinja, Koka 05.jpg|Honden ファイル:Yagawa jinja, Koka 03.jpg|Shrine office === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{commonscat|Yagawa-jinja}} *[http://koka-kanko.org/see/yagawajinjya/ Yagawa Shrine] - Kōka City Tourism Guide {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yagawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yagawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:水口藩]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * やがわじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 258862923 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3160005002972 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 01111448 == Commons category (P373) == * Yagawa-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲南町森尻70', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yagawa-jinja}} [[JA:矢川神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11584115}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2016年6月13日 (月) 20:08 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Yagawa Shrine |画像=[[File:Yagawa jinja, Koka 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}70 Kōnan-chō Morijiri |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|56|6.8|N|136|9|47.6|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yagawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yagawa Shrine''' (やがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan-chō Morijiri|Q86735490|WD=Q86735490|1=Kōnan-chō Morijiri}}, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|title=Yagawa Shrine|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=613|website=|accessdate=2019-10-24|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto is revered as a deity of industry and long healthy life; Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto as a deity for mastery of arts and fulfillment of wishes. Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto is also written as Yagawae-hime / Yagawae-hime. In the genealogy of Ōkuninushi in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}, there is a passage: "{{ill|lt=Kuninintomi-no-Kami|Q48759713|WD=Q48759713|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami}} married {{ill|lt=Ashinadaka-no-Kami|Q55533749|WD=Q55533749|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami}} (also called Yagawae-hime), and their child was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamika-no-Takesawaya-jinumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}." Also, in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} section of the Kojiki, Miyanushi Yagawae-hime appears as one of the emperor's consorts. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era, during construction of {{ill|lt=Shigaraki-no-Miya|Q262438|WD=Q262438|1=Shigaraki Palace}} by {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears first among eight shrines of Kōka District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, and is listed as a small shrine. A 1337 source from the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}} records it as "Soma-no-Kawa-no-Miya," and in the medieval period it gathered support from local warrior-landholders as the sōsha of Ikebara Soma-shō. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}, it was used as a meeting place of the {{ill|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō|Q85882491|WD=Q85882491|1=Kōka ikki}}, functioning as a site strengthening regional cohesion. After the decline of Kōka groups in the {{ill|lt=Kōka-yure|Q6455112|WD=Q6455112|1=Kōga-ryū}} of {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}, ritual structure changed. In {{ill|lt=1652|Q6962|WD=Q6962|1=1652}}, among ujiko villages, 9 villages became ujiko of Shingū Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōnan-chō Shinjī|1=UNKNOWN}}), 6 villages became ujiko of Hiyoshi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Minakuchi-chō Sandaiji|Q86735520|WD=Q86735520|1=Minakuchi-chō Sandaiji}}), and 7 villages (Morijiri, Fukagawa, Hietani, Kuzuraki, Terashō, Sugitani, Shiono) remained ujiko (Yagawa Hongō / Yagawa 7 villages), while another 15 villages were called external ujiko. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was a revered shrine of {{ill|lt=Minakuchi Domain|Q10863504|WD=Q10863504|1=Minakuchi Domain}}. In October {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1842|Q7625|WD=Q7625|1=1842}}), the "Kōka Disturbance" ({{ill|lt=Ōmi Tenpō Uprising|Q11638484|WD=Q11638484|lt=Tenpō uprising|1=Tenpō uprising}}) occurred at this shrine precinct, where tens of thousands of farmers gathered and rose against shogunate land survey policy. Before Meiji it was called Yagawa Daimyōjin and was a broad-area village guardian shrine with a wide ujiko sphere{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016年12月12日|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|pages=171-172}}. Rituals were carried out under {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} by Yagawadera Seijō-in, the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (now extinct). === National historic site === Precincts - designated as a national historic site as one of the {{ill|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō|Q85882491|WD=Q85882491|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō site group|1=Kōka ikki}}{{Cite journal|author=Kōka City|year=2009|title=Understanding the real figure of Kōka warriors who raced through the Sengoku period|url=http://www.city.koka.lg.jp/secure/6711/2009_0601_p02_03.pdf|journal=Kōhō Aikōka|volume=June 1 issue|page=}}. === Cultural properties === === Prefecture-designated cultural properties === * Rōmon gate - said to have been donated in thanks for rainmaking fulfilled for 50 villages in {{ill|lt=Yamabe District|Q1130739|WD=Q1130739|1=Yamabe District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (northern {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}). Originally two-storied, but said to have lost the upper story to weather. Built in {{ill|lt=1482|Q6633|WD=Q6633|1=1482}}. * Paper, light-color paintings "Immortals with game board books" and "Landscape" - by {{ill|lt=Yokoi Kinkoku|Q3122882|WD=Q3122882|1=Yokoi Kinkoku}}. === City-designated cultural properties === * Honden - sangen-sha nagare-zukuri with steel-sheet roof. Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1755|Q7627|WD=Q7627|1=1755}} by Takagi Tajima, a carpenter from Hachiman. * Stone arched bridge (taiko-bashi) - built in {{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}. Oldest stone arched bridge in Kōka{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016年12月12日|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=389}} and largest. * Wooden male deity statue - Heian-period work. === Other === * Wooden female deity statue - Heian-period work. === Gallery === ファイル:Sandō to Yagawa jinja, Koka 04.jpg|Approach ファイル:Yagawa-Shrine.jpg|Torii ファイル:Stone bridge at Yagawa jinja, Koka 05.jpg|Taiko bridge ファイル:Rōmon at Yagawa jinja, Koka 02.jpg|Rōmon ファイル:Yagawa jinja, Koka 05.jpg|Honden ファイル:Yagawa jinja, Koka 03.jpg|Shrine office === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{commonscat|Yagawa-jinja}} *[http://koka-kanko.org/see/yagawajinjya/ Yagawa Shrine] - Kōka City Tourism Guide {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yagawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yagawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:水口藩]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]]

== * Yagawa-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲南町森尻70', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yagawa-jinja}} [[JA:矢川神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11584115}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2016年6月13日 (月) 20:08 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Yagawa Shrine |画像=[[File:Yagawa jinja, Koka 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}70 Kōnan-chō Morijiri |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|56|6.8|N|136|9|47.6|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yagawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yagawa Shrine''' (やがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan-chō Morijiri|Q86735490|WD=Q86735490|1=Kōnan-chō Morijiri}}, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|title=Yagawa Shrine|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=613|website=|accessdate=2019-10-24|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto is revered as a deity of industry and long healthy life; Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto as a deity for mastery of arts and fulfillment of wishes. Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto is also written as Yagawae-hime / Yagawae-hime. In the genealogy of Ōkuninushi in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}, there is a passage: "{{ill|lt=Kuninintomi-no-Kami|Q48759713|WD=Q48759713|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami}} married {{ill|lt=Ashinadaka-no-Kami|Q55533749|WD=Q55533749|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami}} (also called Yagawae-hime), and their child was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamika-no-Takesawaya-jinumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}." Also, in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} section of the Kojiki, Miyanushi Yagawae-hime appears as one of the emperor's consorts. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era, during construction of {{ill|lt=Shigaraki-no-Miya|Q262438|WD=Q262438|1=Shigaraki Palace}} by {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears first among eight shrines of Kōka District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, and is listed as a small shrine. A 1337 source from the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}} records it as "Soma-no-Kawa-no-Miya," and in the medieval period it gathered support from local warrior-landholders as the sōsha of Ikebara Soma-shō. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}, it was used as a meeting place of the {{ill|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō|Q85882491|WD=Q85882491|1=Kōka ikki}}, functioning as a site strengthening regional cohesion. After the decline of Kōka groups in the {{ill|lt=Kōka-yure|Q6455112|WD=Q6455112|1=Kōga-ryū}} of {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}, ritual structure changed. In {{ill|lt=1652|Q6962|WD=Q6962|1=1652}}, among ujiko villages, 9 villages became ujiko of Shingū Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōnan-chō Shinjī|1=UNKNOWN}}), 6 villages became ujiko of Hiyoshi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Minakuchi-chō Sandaiji|Q86735520|WD=Q86735520|1=Minakuchi-chō Sandaiji}}), and 7 villages (Morijiri, Fukagawa, Hietani, Kuzuraki, Terashō, Sugitani, Shiono) remained ujiko (Yagawa Hongō / Yagawa 7 villages), while another 15 villages were called external ujiko. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was a revered shrine of {{ill|lt=Minakuchi Domain|Q10863504|WD=Q10863504|1=Minakuchi Domain}}. In October {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1842|Q7625|WD=Q7625|1=1842}}), the "Kōka Disturbance" ({{ill|lt=Ōmi Tenpō Uprising|Q11638484|WD=Q11638484|lt=Tenpō uprising|1=Tenpō uprising}}) occurred at this shrine precinct, where tens of thousands of farmers gathered and rose against shogunate land survey policy. Before Meiji it was called Yagawa Daimyōjin and was a broad-area village guardian shrine with a wide ujiko sphere{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016年12月12日|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|pages=171-172}}. Rituals were carried out under {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} by Yagawadera Seijō-in, the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (now extinct). === National historic site === Precincts - designated as a national historic site as one of the {{ill|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō|Q85882491|WD=Q85882491|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō site group|1=Kōka ikki}}{{Cite journal|author=Kōka City|year=2009|title=Understanding the real figure of Kōka warriors who raced through the Sengoku period|url=http://www.city.koka.lg.jp/secure/6711/2009_0601_p02_03.pdf|journal=Kōhō Aikōka|volume=June 1 issue|page=}}. === Cultural properties === === Prefecture-designated cultural properties === * Rōmon gate - said to have been donated in thanks for rainmaking fulfilled for 50 villages in {{ill|lt=Yamabe District|Q1130739|WD=Q1130739|1=Yamabe District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (northern {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}). Originally two-storied, but said to have lost the upper story to weather. Built in {{ill|lt=1482|Q6633|WD=Q6633|1=1482}}. * Paper, light-color paintings "Immortals with game board books" and "Landscape" - by {{ill|lt=Yokoi Kinkoku|Q3122882|WD=Q3122882|1=Yokoi Kinkoku}}. === City-designated cultural properties === * Honden - sangen-sha nagare-zukuri with steel-sheet roof. Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1755|Q7627|WD=Q7627|1=1755}} by Takagi Tajima, a carpenter from Hachiman. * Stone arched bridge (taiko-bashi) - built in {{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}. Oldest stone arched bridge in Kōka{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016年12月12日|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=389}} and largest. * Wooden male deity statue - Heian-period work. === Other === * Wooden female deity statue - Heian-period work. === Gallery === ファイル:Sandō to Yagawa jinja, Koka 04.jpg|Approach ファイル:Yagawa-Shrine.jpg|Torii ファイル:Stone bridge at Yagawa jinja, Koka 05.jpg|Taiko bridge ファイル:Rōmon at Yagawa jinja, Koka 02.jpg|Rōmon ファイル:Yagawa jinja, Koka 05.jpg|Honden ファイル:Yagawa jinja, Koka 03.jpg|Shrine office === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{commonscat|Yagawa-jinja}} *[http://koka-kanko.org/see/yagawajinjya/ Yagawa Shrine] - Kōka City Tourism Guide {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yagawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yagawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:水口藩]] [[Category:甲賀市の建築物]]

== * Yagawa-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲南町森尻70', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yagawa-jinja}} [[JA:矢川神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11584115}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2016年6月13日 (月) 20:08 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Yagawa Shrine |画像=[[File:Yagawa jinja, Koka 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}70 Kōnan-chō Morijiri |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|56|6.8|N|136|9|47.6|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yagawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yagawa Shrine''' (やがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan-chō Morijiri|Q86735490|WD=Q86735490|1=Kōnan-chō Morijiri}}, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|title=Yagawa Shrine|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=613|website=|accessdate=2019-10-24|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto is revered as a deity of industry and long healthy life; Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto as a deity for mastery of arts and fulfillment of wishes. Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto is also written as Yagawae-hime / Yagawae-hime. In the genealogy of Ōkuninushi in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}, there is a passage: "{{ill|lt=Kuninintomi-no-Kami|Q48759713|WD=Q48759713|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami}} married {{ill|lt=Ashinadaka-no-Kami|Q55533749|WD=Q55533749|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami}} (also called Yagawae-hime), and their child was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamika-no-Takesawaya-jinumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}." Also, in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} section of the Kojiki, Miyanushi Yagawae-hime appears as one of the emperor's consorts. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era, during construction of {{ill|lt=Shigaraki-no-Miya|Q262438|WD=Q262438|1=Shigaraki Palace}} by {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears first among eight shrines of Kōka District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, and is listed as a small shrine. A 1337 source from the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}} records it as "Soma-no-Kawa-no-Miya," and in the medieval period it gathered support from local warrior-landholders as the sōsha of Ikebara Soma-shō. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}, it was used as a meeting place of the {{ill|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō|Q85882491|WD=Q85882491|1=Kōka ikki}}, functioning as a site strengthening regional cohesion. After the decline of Kōka groups in the {{ill|lt=Kōka-yure|Q6455112|WD=Q6455112|1=Kōga-ryū}} of {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}, ritual structure changed. In {{ill|lt=1652|Q6962|WD=Q6962|1=1652}}, among ujiko villages, 9 villages became ujiko of Shingū Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōnan-chō Shinjī|1=UNKNOWN}}), 6 villages became ujiko of Hiyoshi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Minakuchi-chō Sandaiji|Q86735520|WD=Q86735520|1=Minakuchi-chō Sandaiji}}), and 7 villages (Morijiri, Fukagawa, Hietani, Kuzuraki, Terashō, Sugitani, Shiono) remained ujiko (Yagawa Hongō / Yagawa 7 villages), while another 15 villages were called external ujiko. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was a revered shrine of {{ill|lt=Minakuchi Domain|Q10863504|WD=Q10863504|1=Minakuchi Domain}}. In October {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1842|Q7625|WD=Q7625|1=1842}}), the "Kōka Disturbance" ({{ill|lt=Ōmi Tenpō Uprising|Q11638484|WD=Q11638484|lt=Tenpō uprising|1=Tenpō uprising}}) occurred at this shrine precinct, where tens of thousands of farmers gathered and rose against shogunate land survey policy. Before Meiji it was called Yagawa Daimyōjin and was a broad-area village guardian shrine with a wide ujiko sphere{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016年12月12日|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|pages=171-172}}. Rituals were carried out under {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} by Yagawadera Seijō-in, the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (now extinct). === National historic site === Precincts - designated as a national historic site as one of the {{ill|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō|Q85882491|WD=Q85882491|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō site group|1=Kōka ikki}}{{Cite journal|author=Kōka City|year=2009|title=Understanding the real figure of Kōka warriors who raced through the Sengoku period|url=http://www.city.koka.lg.jp/secure/6711/2009_0601_p02_03.pdf|journal=Kōhō Aikōka|volume=June 1 issue|page=}}. === Cultural properties === === Prefecture-designated cultural properties === * Rōmon gate - said to have been donated in thanks for rainmaking fulfilled for 50 villages in {{ill|lt=Yamabe District|Q1130739|WD=Q1130739|1=Yamabe District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (northern {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}). Originally two-storied, but said to have lost the upper story to weather. Built in {{ill|lt=1482|Q6633|WD=Q6633|1=1482}}. * Paper, light-color paintings "Immortals with game board books" and "Landscape" - by {{ill|lt=Yokoi Kinkoku|Q3122882|WD=Q3122882|1=Yokoi Kinkoku}}. === City-designated cultural properties === * Honden - sangen-sha nagare-zukuri with steel-sheet roof. Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1755|Q7627|WD=Q7627|1=1755}} by Takagi Tajima, a carpenter from Hachiman. * Stone arched bridge (taiko-bashi) - built in {{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}. Oldest stone arched bridge in Kōka{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016年12月12日|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=389}} and largest. * Wooden male deity statue - Heian-period work. === Other === * Wooden female deity statue - Heian-period work. === Gallery === ファイル:Sandō to Yagawa jinja, Koka 04.jpg|Approach ファイル:Yagawa-Shrine.jpg|Torii ファイル:Stone bridge at Yagawa jinja, Koka 05.jpg|Taiko bridge ファイル:Rōmon at Yagawa jinja, Koka 02.jpg|Rōmon ファイル:Yagawa jinja, Koka 05.jpg|Honden ファイル:Yagawa jinja, Koka 03.jpg|Shrine office === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{commonscat|Yagawa-jinja}} *[http://koka-kanko.org/see/yagawajinjya/ Yagawa Shrine] - Kōka City Tourism Guide {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yagawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yagawa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:水口藩]] [[Category:Buildings in Koka, Shiga]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * やがわじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 258862923 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3160005002972 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 01111448 == Commons category (P373) == * Yagawa-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲南町森尻70', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yagawa-jinja}} [[JA:矢川神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11584115}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2016年6月13日 (月) 20:08 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Yagawa Shrine |画像=[[File:Yagawa jinja, Koka 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}70 Kōnan-chō Morijiri |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|56|6.8|N|136|9|47.6|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yagawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yagawa Shrine''' (やがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan-chō Morijiri|Q86735490|WD=Q86735490|1=Kōnan-chō Morijiri}}, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|title=Yagawa Shrine|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=613|website=|accessdate=2019-10-24|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto is revered as a deity of industry and long healthy life; Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto as a deity for mastery of arts and fulfillment of wishes. Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto is also written as Yagawae-hime / Yagawae-hime. In the genealogy of Ōkuninushi in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}, there is a passage: "{{ill|lt=Kuninintomi-no-Kami|Q48759713|WD=Q48759713|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami}} married {{ill|lt=Ashinadaka-no-Kami|Q55533749|WD=Q55533749|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami}} (also called Yagawae-hime), and their child was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamika-no-Takesawaya-jinumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}." Also, in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} section of the Kojiki, Miyanushi Yagawae-hime appears as one of the emperor's consorts. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era, during construction of {{ill|lt=Shigaraki-no-Miya|Q262438|WD=Q262438|1=Shigaraki Palace}} by {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears first among eight shrines of Kōka District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, and is listed as a small shrine. A 1337 source from the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}} records it as "Soma-no-Kawa-no-Miya," and in the medieval period it gathered support from local warrior-landholders as the sōsha of Ikebara Soma-shō. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}, it was used as a meeting place of the {{ill|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō|Q85882491|WD=Q85882491|1=Kōka ikki}}, functioning as a site strengthening regional cohesion. After the decline of Kōka groups in the {{ill|lt=Kōka-yure|Q6455112|WD=Q6455112|1=Kōga-ryū}} of {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}, ritual structure changed. In {{ill|lt=1652|Q6962|WD=Q6962|1=1652}}, among ujiko villages, 9 villages became ujiko of Shingū Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōnan-chō Shinjī|1=UNKNOWN}}), 6 villages became ujiko of Hiyoshi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Minakuchi-chō Sandaiji|Q86735520|WD=Q86735520|1=Minakuchi-chō Sandaiji}}), and 7 villages (Morijiri, Fukagawa, Hietani, Kuzuraki, Terashō, Sugitani, Shiono) remained ujiko (Yagawa Hongō / Yagawa 7 villages), while another 15 villages were called external ujiko. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was a revered shrine of {{ill|lt=Minakuchi Domain|Q10863504|WD=Q10863504|1=Minakuchi Domain}}. In October {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1842|Q7625|WD=Q7625|1=1842}}), the "Kōka Disturbance" ({{ill|lt=Ōmi Tenpō Uprising|Q11638484|WD=Q11638484|lt=Tenpō uprising|1=Tenpō uprising}}) occurred at this shrine precinct, where tens of thousands of farmers gathered and rose against shogunate land survey policy. Before Meiji it was called Yagawa Daimyōjin and was a broad-area village guardian shrine with a wide ujiko sphere{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016年12月12日|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|pages=171-172}}. Rituals were carried out under {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} by Yagawadera Seijō-in, the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (now extinct). === National historic site === Precincts - designated as a national historic site as one of the {{ill|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō|Q85882491|WD=Q85882491|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō site group|1=Kōka ikki}}{{Cite journal|author=Kōka City|year=2009|title=Understanding the real figure of Kōka warriors who raced through the Sengoku period|url=http://www.city.koka.lg.jp/secure/6711/2009_0601_p02_03.pdf|journal=Kōhō Aikōka|volume=June 1 issue|page=}}. === Cultural properties === === Prefecture-designated cultural properties === * Rōmon gate - said to have been donated in thanks for rainmaking fulfilled for 50 villages in {{ill|lt=Yamabe District|Q1130739|WD=Q1130739|1=Yamabe District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (northern {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}). Originally two-storied, but said to have lost the upper story to weather. Built in {{ill|lt=1482|Q6633|WD=Q6633|1=1482}}. * Paper, light-color paintings "Immortals with game board books" and "Landscape" - by {{ill|lt=Yokoi Kinkoku|Q3122882|WD=Q3122882|1=Yokoi Kinkoku}}. === City-designated cultural properties === * Honden - sangen-sha nagare-zukuri with steel-sheet roof. Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1755|Q7627|WD=Q7627|1=1755}} by Takagi Tajima, a carpenter from Hachiman. * Stone arched bridge (taiko-bashi) - built in {{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}. Oldest stone arched bridge in Kōka{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016年12月12日|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=389}} and largest. * Wooden male deity statue - Heian-period work. === Other === * Wooden female deity statue - Heian-period work. === Gallery === ファイル:Sandō to Yagawa jinja, Koka 04.jpg|Approach ファイル:Yagawa-Shrine.jpg|Torii ファイル:Stone bridge at Yagawa jinja, Koka 05.jpg|Taiko bridge ファイル:Rōmon at Yagawa jinja, Koka 02.jpg|Rōmon ファイル:Yagawa jinja, Koka 05.jpg|Honden ファイル:Yagawa jinja, Koka 03.jpg|Shrine office === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{commonscat|Yagawa-jinja}} *[http://koka-kanko.org/see/yagawajinjya/ Yagawa Shrine] - Kōka City Tourism Guide {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yagawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yagawa Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:水口藩]] [[Category:Buildings in Koka, Shiga]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * やがわじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 258862923 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3160005002972 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 01111448 == Commons category (P373) == * Yagawa-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県甲賀市甲南町森尻70', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yagawa-jinja}} [[JA:矢川神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11584115}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2016年6月13日 (月) 20:08 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=Yagawa Shrine |画像=[[File:Yagawa jinja, Koka 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}70 Kōnan-chō Morijiri |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|56|6.8|N|136|9|47.6|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Yagawa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yagawa Shrine''' (やがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōnan-chō Morijiri|Q86735490|WD=Q86735490|1=Kōnan-chō Morijiri}}, {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q847584|WD=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto{{Cite web|和書|title=Yagawa Shrine|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=613|website=|accessdate=2019-10-24|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office}}. Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto is revered as a deity of industry and long healthy life; Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto as a deity for mastery of arts and fulfillment of wishes. Yagawa-e-hime-no-Mikoto is also written as Yagawae-hime / Yagawae-hime. In the genealogy of Ōkuninushi in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}, there is a passage: "{{ill|lt=Kuninintomi-no-Kami|Q48759713|WD=Q48759713|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami}} married {{ill|lt=Ashinadaka-no-Kami|Q55533749|WD=Q55533749|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami}} (also called Yagawae-hime), and their child was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamika-no-Takesawaya-jinumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}." Also, in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} section of the Kojiki, Miyanushi Yagawae-hime appears as one of the emperor's consorts. === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era, during construction of {{ill|lt=Shigaraki-no-Miya|Q262438|WD=Q262438|1=Shigaraki Palace}} by {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears first among eight shrines of Kōka District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, and is listed as a small shrine. A 1337 source from the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}} records it as "Soma-no-Kawa-no-Miya," and in the medieval period it gathered support from local warrior-landholders as the sōsha of Ikebara Soma-shō. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}, it was used as a meeting place of the {{ill|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō|Q85882491|WD=Q85882491|1=Kōka ikki}}, functioning as a site strengthening regional cohesion. After the decline of Kōka groups in the {{ill|lt=Kōka-yure|Q6455112|WD=Q6455112|1=Kōga-ryū}} of {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}, ritual structure changed. In {{ill|lt=1652|Q6962|WD=Q6962|1=1652}}, among ujiko villages, 9 villages became ujiko of Shingū Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōnan-chō Shinjī|1=UNKNOWN}}), 6 villages became ujiko of Hiyoshi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Minakuchi-chō Sandaiji|Q86735520|WD=Q86735520|1=Minakuchi-chō Sandaiji}}), and 7 villages (Morijiri, Fukagawa, Hietani, Kuzuraki, Terashō, Sugitani, Shiono) remained ujiko (Yagawa Hongō / Yagawa 7 villages), while another 15 villages were called external ujiko. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was a revered shrine of {{ill|lt=Minakuchi Domain|Q10863504|WD=Q10863504|1=Minakuchi Domain}}. In October {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1842|Q7625|WD=Q7625|1=1842}}), the "Kōka Disturbance" ({{ill|lt=Ōmi Tenpō Uprising|Q11638484|WD=Q11638484|lt=Tenpō uprising|1=Tenpō uprising}}) occurred at this shrine precinct, where tens of thousands of farmers gathered and rose against shogunate land survey policy. Before Meiji it was called Yagawa Daimyōjin and was a broad-area village guardian shrine with a wide ujiko sphere{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016年12月12日|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|pages=171-172}}. Rituals were carried out under {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} by Yagawadera Seijō-in, the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (now extinct). === National historic site === Precincts - designated as a national historic site as one of the {{ill|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō|Q85882491|WD=Q85882491|lt=Kōka-gun Chūsō site group|1=Kōka ikki}}{{Cite journal|author=Kōka City|year=2009|title=Understanding the real figure of Kōka warriors who raced through the Sengoku period|url=http://www.city.koka.lg.jp/secure/6711/2009_0601_p02_03.pdf|journal=Kōhō Aikōka|volume=June 1 issue|page=}}. === Cultural properties === === Prefecture-designated cultural properties === * Rōmon gate - said to have been donated in thanks for rainmaking fulfilled for 50 villages in {{ill|lt=Yamabe District|Q1130739|WD=Q1130739|1=Yamabe District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (northern {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}). Originally two-storied, but said to have lost the upper story to weather. Built in {{ill|lt=1482|Q6633|WD=Q6633|1=1482}}. * Paper, light-color paintings "Immortals with game board books" and "Landscape" - by {{ill|lt=Yokoi Kinkoku|Q3122882|WD=Q3122882|1=Yokoi Kinkoku}}. === City-designated cultural properties === * Honden - sangen-sha nagare-zukuri with steel-sheet roof. Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1755|Q7627|WD=Q7627|1=1755}} by Takagi Tajima, a carpenter from Hachiman. * Stone arched bridge (taiko-bashi) - built in {{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}. Oldest stone arched bridge in Kōka{{Cite book|title=Kōka City History Vol.8 Kōka City Encyclopedia|date=2016年12月12日|year=2016|publisher=Kōka City|page=389}} and largest. * Wooden male deity statue - Heian-period work. === Other === * Wooden female deity statue - Heian-period work. === Gallery === ファイル:Sandō to Yagawa jinja, Koka 04.jpg|Approach ファイル:Yagawa-Shrine.jpg|Torii ファイル:Stone bridge at Yagawa jinja, Koka 05.jpg|Taiko bridge ファイル:Rōmon at Yagawa jinja, Koka 02.jpg|Rōmon ファイル:Yagawa jinja, Koka 05.jpg|Honden ファイル:Yagawa jinja, Koka 03.jpg|Shrine office === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{commonscat|Yagawa-jinja}} *[http://koka-kanko.org/see/yagawajinjya/ Yagawa Shrine] - Kōka City Tourism Guide {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yagawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yagawa Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:甲賀市の歴史]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:水口藩]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Koka, Shiga]]
